element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Five color-coded species with distinct abilities (flight, swimming, lava/ice grip)
Hidden eggs yield keys for optional content

typical_relief_triggers: Finding food items to restore energy
typical_tension_triggers: Dinosaur partner lost on enemy hit
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant low-level tension from energy depletion timer creates sustained urgency; relieved through strategic item collection and dinosaur acquisition; escalates in advanced stages with more challenging platforming sections

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Eggs hidden throughout levels contain secrets; keys unlock optional areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden eggs accessible via weapon use or jumping; secret areas behind locked doors require key collection

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to movement, jumping, and energy management; safe zones with abundant food items
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of water sections, more complex platforming, hazardous items appear; dinosaur partners become essential
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced stages become very challenging; specialized environmental hazards (lava, ice) require specific dinosaur selection; tighter enemy placement and platform sequences
notable_spikes: Advanced stages noted by Nintendo Power reviewer as 'very challenging'
Environments requiring specific dinosaur abilities (lava/ice sections)
Later island sequences with increased enemy density

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight islands serve as thematic level groupings; each island represents a different environment requiring adaptation of strategy and dinosaur selection
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Island 1 (introductory), later islands feature specialized terrain

notable_level_patterns.description: Players select weapon type, power-up item (invincibility or time-stop), and dinosaur partner before entering each level; creates strategic decision-making and replayability
Eggs scattered throughout levels contain weapons, food, keys, dinosaur partners, and occasional penalties; encourages exploration and offers non-linear progression options
Later levels feature terrain requiring specific dinosaur abilities (lava-resistant, ice-grip, flight, swimming); forces replaying with different partner selections

sources_used: Wikipedia - Adventure Island 3 (gameplay, development, release, reception sections)
The Cutting Room Floor - Hudson's Adventure Island III (stage select, series context)
